ref: Later that same day Isaac's servants came and said, “We've struck water!”
text: Ni eba mli nakai gbĩ le̱ noṅṅ ake̱ Isak tšũdši le̱ ha ni ame̱bagbale̱ nubu ni ame̱dše le̱ he sane ake̱: Wo̱yanina nu.
translated: He did not say anything to Isaac, but he said to them, "I will give you a sign."

ref: Isaac took Rebekah into the tent where his mother had lived before she died, and Rebekah became his wife. He loved her and was comforted over the loss of his mother.
text: Ni Isak ke̱le̱ ba enye Sara bū le̱ mli; ni eṅõ Rebeka ni ebatšṍ eṅã, ni esumo̱le̱. Ni Isak tšé̱ etšui eṅmẽ emli ye̱ *enye gbĕle le̱ se̱. *
translated: And Isaac said to her, "Sara, my wife, you are my mother; and Rebekah, my mother, she is my mother, and she is my mother. And Isaac said to her, "My mother, you are my mother."

ref: he put him in charge of the other prisoners and of everything that was done in the jail.
text: Ni gbokle̱foi akwe̱lo̱ le̱ tu gbokle̱foi le̱ ni yo̱ tšũ le̱ṅ le̱ fẽ ewo Iosef deṅ, ni nofẽno ni afeo̱ ye̱ dše̱i le̱, le̱ efeo̱.
translated: And they brought Joseph up and set him before Pharaoh, and Pharaoh said to his servants, "See, this is the one whom God has made ready for me."

ref: Instead, go back to the land where I was born and find a wife for him from among my relatives.”
text: ši moṅ oote mišikpoṅ le̱ no̱ ke̱ miweku le̱ mli ni oyaw̌e̱ yō abahã mibi Isak.
translated: This morning, the young man went to the forest and found a woman there who was weeping. He asked her why she was weeping and she told him that her name was Isak.

ref: I will bring you into the land that I solemnly promised to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, and it will be yours. I am the Lord!”
text: ni mike̱nye aate šikpoṅ ni *miwó minine no̱ ake̱ mike̱-ahã Abraham ke̱ Isak ke̱ Iakob le̱ no̱, ni mike̱-ahãnye ní nyeye no, midši Iehowa le̱! *
translated: For Mike̱nye aate šikpoṅ ni *miwó minine no̱ ake̱ mike̱-ahã Abraham ke̱ Isak ke̱ Iakob le̱ no̱, ni mike̱-ahãnye ní nyeye no, midši Iehowa le̱!

ref: When they arrived, Isaac asked, “Why are you here? Didn't you send me away because you hated me?”
text: Ni Isak kē̱ame̱ ake̱: Meni bewo̱ nyebaä miṅõ, nye ni nyenye̱o̱mi, ni nyeſiemi ye̱ nyemase̱i le̱?
translated: Isak, what is your name? I don't know you, I haven't seen you before, have we met somewhere?

ref: While Joseph was in prison, both the king's personal servant and his chief cook made the king angry. So he had them thrown into the same prison with Joseph.
text: ni eyawoame̱ tšũṅ ye̱ oblafoi atše̱ le̱ šĩa le̱, ye̱ gbokle̱foi atšũ le̱ṅ, heni aṅõ *Iosef awo le̱. *
translated: This is the story of Joseph.

ref: He cooked the tasty food, brought it to his father, and said, “Father, please sit up and eat the meat I have brought you, so you can give me your blessing.”
text: ni le̱ hũ efe niyenii ke̱ ṅõmo̱ ni eke̱-bahã etše̱, ni ekē̱ etše̱ ake̱: Hã mitše̱ ate ši, ni eye ehi mĩle̱lo le̱ eko, koni osusuma adšō̱mi.
translated: You have a good heart, you are kind and generous, and you are always willing to help. You are a true friend, and I am grateful for your friendship.

ref: Abram moved from place to place in the Southern Desert. And finally, he went north and set up his tents between Bethel and Ai,
text: Ni ekã egbe̱fā̃i le̱ ahe ke̱-dše̱ wuoyigbe̱ le̱ ke̱-te Betel tō̃, ke̱-te heni eši ebū le̱ ye̱ tšutšu le̱, ye̱ Betel ke̱ Ai teṅ,
translated: And he said to them, "I will show you something, something that will happen in the future, in the days of the son of man.  And you will see the Son of Man coming in the clouds of heaven, with power and great glory.  And he will send his angels with a loud trumpet call, and they will gather his elect from the four winds, from one end of heaven to the other."

ref: Our Lord, I am waiting for you to save us.
text: Iehowa, owalaheremo̱ le̱ gbe̱ mikwe̱o̱ ne̱! Lal.
translated: Jehovah, you are great and mighty! Hallelujah.

ref: But you must bring your youngest brother to me. Then I'll know that you are telling the truth, and you won't be put to death. Joseph's brothers agreed
text: ni nyeke̱ nyenyemi gbekẽ le̱ abaä miṅõ, no ake̱-ana nyewiemo̱i le̱ ano̱kwale, koni nyekagboi. Ni ame̱fe nakai.
translated: I will not go to the village, I will not go to the river, I will not go to the bush. I will stay here.

ref: Then the people came to him and said: Sir, there's no way we can hide the truth from you. We are broke, and we don't have any more animals. We have nothing left except ourselves and our land.
text: Beni nakai afi le̱ ho le̱, ame̱ba eṅṍ ye̱ afi ni dši enyo̱ le̱ no̱, ni ame̱bakē̱le̱ ake̱: Wo̱ṅõ-tēṅ wo̱nuṅtšo̱ ake̱ wo̱šika ke̱ koloi kui le̱ fẽ etã, ni ake̱-ba wo̱nuṅtšo̱ ṅõ; eſe̱ko nokonoko ye̱ wo̱nuṅtšo̱ hĩe doṅṅ, ake̱ dša wo̱gbomotšei ke̱ wo̱šikpõdši.
translated: I have not seen him, but I heard that he is coming, and I am afraid of him, because he is a powerful man: He will come with his people and destroy everything, and he will leave nothing behind; he will kill everyone, and he will take all the possessions.

ref: Then tell him that I have said, “Israel is my first-born son,
text: Ni okē̱ Farao ake̱: Bo̱ni Iehowa kē̱ ne̱; *Mibi, mikromo̱bi dši Israel; *Ier.
translated: Thus says the Lord God: "I will bring down Pharaoh and his army; I will make the Egyptians know that I am the Lord."

ref: If the woman refuses to come along, you don't have to keep this promise. But don't ever take my son back there.”
text: Ni ke̱dši yö le̱ sumo̱o̱ ake̱ eenyie ose̱ le̱, no le̱ obe edše mikitã ne̱ mli; mibi le̱ ke̱ke̱ ni oke̱le̱ akaya dše̱i doṅṅ.
translated: You are going to the place where the sun sets, and you will not return; you will be there until you die.

ref: But his father said, “Son, I know what I am doing. It's true that Manasseh's family will someday become a great nation. But Ephraim will be even greater than Manasseh, because his descendants will become many great nations.”
text: Ni etše ekplẽẽ ni ekē̱: Mile, mibi, mile; le̱ hũ ehatšṍ maṅ, ebatšṍ kpeteṅkple moṅ; ši kẽle̱ enyemi gbekẽ le̱ batšṍ kpeteṅkple fèle̱, ni eseši aatšṍ mãdši babaō.
translated: I am telling you what I saw: Mile, my friend, Mile; he was sitting there, he was eating kpeteṅkple; he was eating it with his hands, he was eating it with his hands, and he was saying "I am eating my father's food".

ref: There has already been a famine for two years, and for five more years no one will plow fields or harvest grain.
text: Ši afii enyo̱ ne̱ ní hõmo̱ ba šikpoṅ le̱ no̱, ni eſe̱ afii enumo̱ hũ lolo ni hũmo̱ lo kpamo̱ be̱ mli.
translated: This is the way we do it, we do not go to the market, we only do it at home.

ref: They kept on wrestling until the man said, “Let go of me! It's almost daylight.” “You can't go until you bless me,” Jacob replied.
text: Ni beni ena ake̱ enyẽẽle̱ le̱, eta egboṅ talo̱ le̱ he, ni Iakob gboṅ talo̱ le̱ hwãṅ beni eke̱le̱ nõo̱ le̱.
translated: My father gave me a gift, and I said to him, "Father, I will give you a gift in return."

ref: Jacob replied, “I am Esau, your first-born, and I have done what you told me. Please sit up and eat the meat I have brought. Then you can give me your blessing.”
text: Ni Iakob kē̱ etše̱ ake̱: Mi, Esau, okromo̱bi le̱ṅ; mife be̱ni okē̱mi le̱. Ofai ne̱, te ši otá ši, ni oye mimĩle̱lo le̱ eko, koni osusuma adšō̱mi!
translated: Jacob said to Esau: "My brother, Esau, I am your servant; I have been your servant. Now, if you please, forgive me, and accept my gift, so that I may be in your favor!

ref: Laban named the pile of rocks Jegar Sahadutha. But Jacob named it Galeed.
text: Ni Laban wole̱ Iegar-Sahaduta; ši Iakob le̱ ewole̱ Galed.
translated: Ni Laban went to Iegar-Sahaduta; and Jacob went to Galed.

ref: We were out in the field, tying up bundles of wheat. Suddenly my bundle stood up, and your bundles gathered around and bowed down to it.”
text: Ši na, wo̱mĩfĩmo̱ ṅmā̃kei ye̱ ṅmõši, ni na, mike le̱ te ši damo̱ ši, ni na, nyekei le̱ babo̱le mike le̱ ni ame̱kotole̱.
translated: This is, I am not going to say it, I will not say it, I will not say it, I will not say it, I will not say it.

ref: After Joseph was born, Jacob said to Laban, “Release me from our agreement and let me return to my own country.
text: Ni eba mli ake, beni Rahel efo̱ Iosef se̱ le̱, Iakob kē̱ Laban akē̱: Womi gbe̱, koni maya midientše̱ midšē̱he ke̱ mišikpoṅ no̱.
translated: Then Jacob said to Laban, "I have been here with you for twenty years, and I have served you for twenty years. Now, it is time for me to return to my own land."

ref: to kill a wild animal and cook some tasty food for him before he dies. Your father said this because he wants to bless your brother with the Lord as his witness.
text: Yatao mĩle̱lo, ni ofele̱ niyenii ke̱ ṅõmo̱ obahãmi, koni maye, ni madšō̱o ye̱ Iehowa hĩe, dãni magbo.
translated: I have heard, and I have listened, I have understood, I will do it, I will not be slow, I will do it as Jehovah has commanded me.

ref: Then she said, “That Hebrew slave of yours tried to rape me!
text: Ni edšadše ne̱ke̱ wiemo̱i ne̱ noṅṅ etšõle̱ ake̱: Nyṍṅnū Hebrinyo le̱ ni ba wo̱ṅõ le̱ ba ní ebaye mihe fe̱o.
translated: You have heard that it was said to those of old, 'You shall not murder, and whoever murders shall be liable to judgment.'

ref: Later, when Hagar knew she was going to have a baby, she became proud and treated Sarai hatefully.
text: Ni eba Hagar ṅõ, ni ená mũsũ; ši beni ena ake̱ ehĩe mũsũ le̱, ewura le̱ he guo̱ gbo ye̱ ehĩe.
translated: You are not Hagar, you are my mother; this one is my son, I have given him to you to nurse, so that you may nurse him.

ref: Finally, the men in charge of the slaves went to the king and said, “Why are you treating us like this?
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni Israelbii le̱ ano̱kwe̱lo̱i le̱ ba ni ame̱bablo̱ ame̱wo Farao ame̱kē̱: Mẽhewo̱ oke̱ otšũdši yeo̱ ne̱ke̱?
translated: What did you say to the Pharaoh, that he would let the Israelites go?
